RULING: PLAINTIFFS' NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE FOR TRIAL THE CASES AGAINST KAISER GYPSUM COMPANY, INC. AND HANSON PERMANENTE CEMENT, INC. FORMERLY KNOWN AS KAISER CEMENT CORPORATION
Parties stipulated to have all matters heard simultaneously. Hearing held, argued and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiffs' motion to consolidate for trial the cases against Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc. and Hanson Permanente Cement, Inc. formerly known as Kaiser Cement corporation, into the following subgroups: 1) wrongful death/mesothelioma; 2) living/lung cancer: 3) wrongful death/lung cancer; 4) living/other cancer; 5) wrongful death/other cancer; 6) living/asbestosis; 7) wrongful death/asbestosis; 8) living/pleural disease, is denied. The actions do not involve sufficient common questions of law or fact, economy and convenience would not be served by a joint trial, and consolidation may prejudice one or both of the defendants. Prevailing party is to submit a proposed order in compliance with C.R.C. 3.1312. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. Application For Entry Of Default Judgment Against Defendant Allied Plasterers, Inc.
Off Calendar Dec-04-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing as to defendant Allied Plasterers, Inc.
Dept. 514. Judge: Eric R. Fleming. Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, Reporter: Brittni Christopher, CSR #10791. Telephone: 415-551-3745. Email: bchristopher@sftc.org.

Andrew Chew, Esq. appeared for plaintiffs Susan Hetzel, as Successor-in-Interest to and as Wrongful Death Heir of James Hetzel, Deceased; and Terri Salmi, Rochelle Gallegos and Charlene Romero, as Wrongful Death Heirs of James Hetzel, Deceased.
No appearance by defaulted defendant Allied Plasters, Inc.
Plaintiff's complaint for Survival/Wrongful Death was filed on May 5, 2015.
Amendment to susbsitute Allied Plaster, Inc. for DOE 302 was filed on September 23, 2015.
Summons, Complaint & Statement of Damages was personally served on October 12, 2015.
Statement of Damages was filed on December 15, 2015.
Default as to defendant Allied Plasterers, Inc. was entered on December 15, 2015.
Order waiving witness appearance at default prove-up hearing was filed on November 5, 2018.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiffs Susan Hetzel, as Successor-in-Interest to and as Wrongful Death Heir of James Hetzel, Deceased; and Terri Salmi, Rochelle Gallegos and Charlene Romero, as Wrongful Death Heirs of James Hetzel, Deceased, and against the Defaulted Defendant Allied Plasterers, Inc., as follows:

Richard Grant of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Susan Hetzel, as Successor-in-Interest to and as Wrongful Death Heir of James Hetzel, deceased, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT HENNESSY INDUSTRIES, LLC'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Hennessy Industries, LLC's demurrer to the third and fourth causes of action in plaintiffs' First Amended Complaint is sustained without leave to amend. The complaint and matters of which this Court takes judicial notice demonstrate that the wrongful death causes of action are barred by the applicable statute of limitations, C.C.P. 340.2(c). The May 5, 2015 complaint did not name Hennessy as a defendant. The First Amended Complaint naming Hennessy as a defendant was filed beyond the limitations period and it does not relate back to the filing of plaintiffs' original complaint. Specifically, the items demonstrate that plaintiffs were not genuinely ignorant of defendant's true name or the facts rendering defendant liable at the time they filed their May 5, 2015 complaint. Defendant's request for judicial notice of Exhibits A through I, to which there is no objection, is granted. Prevailing party to prepare a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312.
